objective: Define the Secret Provisioning UX standards contract for missing-secret and deploy-preflight diagnostics without adding a parallel provisioning tool.
scope_boundary: Issue #509 implements the standards and environment-registry contract only.
Use existing GOV documentation surfaces: STANDARDS.md and docs/ENV_REGISTRY.md.
Do not add validator logic, Pages deploy gate code, runbook scrub changes, or downstream repo writes in this lane.
out_of_scope: No-secret provisioning evidence validator; issue #510 owns that.
Pages deploy gate code/tests/runbook changes; issue #511 owns that.
Governance runbook scrub; issue #512 owns that.
Downstream inventory and follow-up issues; issue #513 owns that.
Secret rotation, value provisioning, or any commit of .env.shared, generated .env files, provider tokens, raw phone numbers, signed URLs, Authorization headers, or credential screenshots.
research_summary: The #509 standards explorer found the right insertion points in existing docs rather than a new tool: add a Secret Provisioning UX section immediately before STANDARDS.md Security Standards, and add a Secret Provisioning UX Contract immediately after docs/ENV_REGISTRY.md Quick Start. Existing precedent in docs/ENV_REGISTRY.md already treats .env.shared as the gitignored SSOT and bootstrap-generated repo env files as generated artifacts; #509 will make that the canonical operator UX contract.

sprints.name: Sprint 1 - Standards contract
sprints.goal: Define missing-secret diagnostics and approved provisioning surfaces in the existing standards and env registry docs.
tasks: Add Secret Provisioning UX to STANDARDS.md before Security Standards.
Add Secret Provisioning UX Contract to docs/ENV_REGISTRY.md after Quick Start.
Document canonical wording for missing, blocked, and ready states.
Keep all examples name-only and value-free.
acceptance_criteria: STANDARDS.md requires missing-secret diagnostics to list variable names only and never print secret values.
STANDARDS.md prohibits inline secret-setting instructions such as export NAME=value, launchctl setenv NAME value, and echo value | gh secret set.
docs/ENV_REGISTRY.md points operators to approved paths: hldpro-governance/.env.shared, bootstrap-generated ignored repo env files, provider vault/dashboard, and GitHub Actions secrets/vars.
docs/ENV_REGISTRY.md includes canonical wording for missing, blocked, and ready states.
No examples include secret values, token placeholders, Authorization headers, signed URLs, raw phone numbers, or generated env file contents.

specialist_reviews.focus: Find existing documentation insertion points and local precedent for issue #509.
specialist_reviews.summary: Recommended extending STANDARDS.md immediately before Security Standards and docs/ENV_REGISTRY.md immediately after Quick Start, preserving existing operator bootstrap flow.

alternate_model_review.reviewer: not requested for scoped documentation contract
alternate_model_review.summary: This lane is a narrow documentation standards contract derived from approved epic #507 planning and a read-only standards explorer. Validator and deploy-gate implementation lanes remain separate.

execution_handoff.approved_scope_summary: Issue #509 may update STANDARDS.md and docs/ENV_REGISTRY.md plus its own structured plan, execution scope, and validation evidence.
execution_handoff.next_execution_step: Merge the issue #509 execution scope as a planning-only PR, then apply STANDARDS.md and docs/ENV_REGISTRY.md changes on the same issue branch name so CI can trust the base scope.
handoff_acceptance_criteria: Execution scope validates with lane claim.
Local CI gate passes before merge.

material_deviation_rules: If issue #509 needs validator, deploy gate, runbook scrub, or downstream repo changes, stop and route to #510, #511, #512, or #513.

If evidence would contain credential values, signed URLs, Authorization headers, raw phone numbers, generated env files, or screenshots containing credentials, replace it with redacted structured evidence before commit.
If the trusted execution scope is not present on base, merge the scope-only planning PR before implementation.
